commands.review_summary:
The PR makes CodexBar status items use `.removalAllowed` and treats AWS Cost Explorer `DataUnavailableException` HTTP 400 responses as empty results.

Reproducibility: Partly/source-only: current main clearly throws for Bedrock non-200 Cost Explorer responses, and the status-item removal risk follows from source inspection. I did not run live AWS or a macOS Command-drag bundle proof, and the PR provides no artifact.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor follow-up and maintainer judgment are needed because proof is missing and the removable status-item behavior has an unresolved recovery/UX decision.

target.title: Fix status item positioning and Bedrock Cost Explorer errors
target.body:
## Description
This PR addresses two minor issues:
1. **Status Item Position:** Added `.removalAllowed` behavior to the `NSStatusItem`. This enables users to `Command`-drag the CodexBar icon to custom positions in their macOS menu bar (e.g., to the left of the battery).
2. **AWS Bedrock Cost Explorer:** Handled `DataUnavailableException` returned by AWS Cost Explorer (HTTP 400). When Cost Explorer is newly enabled or has no data, it now gracefully treats it as $0 usage rather than surfacing an API error to the user.

## Testing
- Verified the status bar item is draggable.
- Verified that providing a Bedrock account with no usage/freshly enabled Cost Explorer logs the unavailability and proceeds cleanly without throwing.
